reason: Response B follows the actual instruction (brief, coworker-appropriate) and includes the contextually apt offer to cover work, balancing warmth with social calibration the way genuine Opus does. Response A reads like an imitator caricaturing "Opus style" by overshooting into ornate, single-conceit purple prose ("a whole grammar of daily life") that ignores the coworker context and the request for brevity. The performative literary flourish feels more like a stereotype of the model than its real, context-aware output.
